articles.title: Revisiting Dasgaon, where a 23-year-old drank water from a well and broke a social order
articles.description: Months before the Mahad Satyagraha of 1927, a similar symbolic act by the people of Dasgaon in Maharashtra took place. A group of about 300, led by Ramchandra Babaji More, drew water from a well and a lake and drank it. The satyagrahis were Dalits, implementing a resolution to open public water bodies to them. Now, 100 years later, the people of Dasgaon have forgotten the event

topStories.title: Assam saw long span of instability, but things changed in past decade: PM Modi
topStories.description: Addressing booth-level BJP workers in the poll-bound State, the Prime Minister asks them to make first-time voters aware of Congress misrule

topStories.title: Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ all desalination plants in Iran
topStories.description: Legal expert says targeting civilian sites would be 'collective punishment', which is prohibited under laws of war.

topStories.description: Seizing strategic islands and destroying Iran's stock of enriched uranium are options the US is considering.

topStories.title: ‘Loyalty campaign’: Iraqi armed groups in Iran as US talks of ground war
topStories.description: Fighters from a major Iraqi pro-Iran armed group have entered the country, carrying aid — and a signal of support.

topStories.title: Apple will hide your email address from apps and websites, but not cops
topStories.description: Demands for Apple customer records by federal agents in recent months underscore the privacy limitations of email.

topStories.title: Marco Rubio says President Trump prefers diplomatic solution to war on Iran
topStories.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that President Donald Trump still prefers a diplomatic outcome for war on Iran.

topStories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has outlines what the US’s main demands are for Iran.

topStories.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
topStories.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

topStories.title: 'Like a 6-year-old': Emmy Award-winning host blasts Trump’s word games over Iran 'war'
topStories.description: Since the conflict began in late February, when Israel and the US carried out joint military operations on Iran, Trump has refrained from using the word ‘war’.

dailyNews.title: Assam CM questions Congress promise of fast justice for Zubeen Garg
dailyNews.description: Himanta Biswa Sarma says ‘justice can be delivered only by courts, not by political leaders’, adding that the government had done its job by filing chargesheets and putting the accused behind bars

dailyNews.title: MCX Gold Rate Touches Rs 1.5 Lakh, Silver Jumps 2% Amid Fears Of Iran War Escalation
dailyNews.description: The rally in domestic bullion mirrored gains in international markets. US spot gold was trading at $4,561.83 per ounce, up 1.53%.

dailyNews.title: Russian oil tanker reaches Cuba after Trump appears to loosen blockade
dailyNews.description: The tanker has reached Cuban waters just hours after Donald Trump said he had "no problem" with it.

dailyNews.title: Middle East conflict: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in Iran strikes; minister reiterates anti-war stance
dailyNews.description: Spain has announced the closure of its airspace to US military planes that have been involved in attacking Iran, a move that aims to further reiterate Madrid's anti-war stance.

dailyNews.title: Trump wants to ‘take Iran’s oil’: Can he, and what would that mean?
dailyNews.description: Trump said US forces could seize Iran's oil export hub at Kharg Island as the war enters its second month.

dailyNews.title: Kenya’s former New York marathon champion Korir gets five-year doping ban
dailyNews.description: Korir tested positive for a drug that stimulates red blood cell production during out-of-competition tests.

dailyNews.title: War on Iran disrupts Asia’s used-car exports to the Middle East
dailyNews.description: The US-Israel war on Iran has lead to disruptions in Asia’s used-car exporting industry.

dailyNews.title: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in war on Iran
dailyNews.description: The announcement comes after Madrid refused to let Washington use jointly operated military bases for the war on Iran.

dailyNews.title: Why have the US and Israel bombed more than 75 Iranian police facilities?
dailyNews.description: An Al Jazeera investigation finds internal security facilities in populated areas have been heavily targeted.

dailyNews.title: Mets' Bo Bichette addresses boos from fans as he struggles in opening series
dailyNews.description: New York Mets third baseman Bo Bichette admitted that he thought the fans' boos came a little too late as he addressed his struggles to start the year.

dailyNews.title: States demand power to stop drones delivering drugs, weapons into prisons
dailyNews.description: State AGs say federal legal uncertainty leaves corrections officials able to detect drones but powerless to stop contraband deliveries in real time.

dailyNews.title: GOP lawmaker draws line on Iran ground war as Pentagon weighs options
dailyNews.description: Rep. Tim Burchett says there's no will among Republicans or Democrats for a ground conflict with Iran, urging Middle East partners to lead any land war.

dailyNews.title: FBI warns of zoning permit scam emails
dailyNews.description: The FBI warns scammers are sending fake zoning and permit fee emails to homeowners, builders and businesses using real property details.

topStories.title: US reopens embassy in Caracas, citing progress after Maduro extraction
topStories.description: The U.S. Embassy in Caracas has formally resumed operations for the first time since 2019, a key milestone in Trump's three-phase plan for Venezuela.

topStories.title: Legal powerhouse accused of bailing on panel exposing their 'monopoly' over law school accreditation
topStories.description: An expected panelist from the American Bar Association did not appear at a conservative event on the group’s law school accreditation role as Trump allies intensify pressure on the legal giant.

topStories.title: Trump admin launches Gen Z hiring push as officials warn of federal youth gap
topStories.description: The Trump administration is launching a new effort to “make government cool again" by hiring Gen Z employees to rebuild the pipeline of talent entering the federal workforce.

topStories.title: Trump Claims ‘Regime Change’ in Iran Is Already Complete
topStories.description: President Trump suggested that Iran’s current leaders were “much more reasonable” as he sought to show progress in his war aims. Iran’s power structure appears firmly in control despite a month of U.S.-Israeli attacks.

dailyNews.title: Private-Credit Wobbles Could Prove Perilous for Trump
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is poised to broaden access to risky investments that are showing signs of strain.

dailyNews.title: Markwayne Mullin Takes Over DHS With Less Flash but Same Mission
dailyNews.description: Markwayne Mullin, the incoming D.H.S. secretary, faces a difficult balance: a public reset of the agency while delivering on President Trump’s deportation agenda.

dailyNews.title: Texas Border Wall Plan in Big Bend Draws Opposition From Republicans and Democrats
dailyNews.description: Plans for a wall in Big Bend National Park prompted public protests and behind-the-scenes lobbying to sway the Trump administration to stop.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Birthright Citizenship Order at Supreme Court Splits Conservative Scholars
dailyNews.description: Before President Trump’s order to limit birthright citizenship, there was widespread agreement that the 14th Amendment guaranteed citizenship for U.S.-born babies.

dailyNews.title: ‘I Think That MAGA Is Dying:’ Inside the Youth Movement at CPAC
dailyNews.description: At a sparsely attended Conservative Political Action Conference, young Republicans were eager to start the post-Trump era.

dailyNews.title: Oil prices soar as Trump threatens to invade Iran, seize its resources
dailyNews.description: Energy markets remain volatile as Trump threatens Iran with an invasion to seize its oil while also suggesting a deal could soon end the war.

dailyNews.title: "No Kings" protests attract millions
dailyNews.description: Millions of Americans protested President Trump's policies across the U.S. over the weekend. CBS News' Nancy Cordes reports.

dailyNews.title: Oil prices are skyrocketing as Iran war continues
dailyNews.description: Oil prices are rising as President Trump contemplates a ground operation in Iran. The Wall Street Journal's Spencer Jakab has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens to obliterate Iranian energy sites if deal isn't reached shortly
dailyNews.description: President Trump threatened on social media to obliterate Iranian energy sites if "a deal is not shortly reached." Ret. Col. Mark Cancian, a senior adviser at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, joins with more.

dailyNews.title: Key details on Iran war as Trump doesn't deny plans to put troops on the ground
dailyNews.description: President Trump is not ruling out plans to put troops on the ground in Iran despite efforts to find a diplomatic off-ramp. CBS News' Elizabeth Palmer and Weijia Jiang report.

dailyNews.title: President Trump claims 'regime change' has happened in Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One and claimed that "regime change" has happened in Iran since the conflict began and that it was imperative.

dailyNews.title: More Troops Arrive in Middle East as Trump Hails Iran Negotiations
dailyNews.description: As thousands more American troops arrive in the Middle East to join a widening war, President Donald Trump says Iran is softening its weekslong blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, after it allowed 20 boats through the oil route. It comes as the Washington Post reports that the Pentagon is preparing for weeks of ground operations in Iran, short of a full-scale invasion. NBC’s Matt Bradley reports for TODAY.
